thesis: Beta-neutral idiosyncratic-volatility low-vol: long lowest-idio-vol, short highest-idio-vol stocks (residual vol vs equal-weight market), neutralizing net market beta to isolate the lottery-preference/arbitrage-asymmetry premium rather than a low-beta tilt; monthly rebalance.

Idiosyncratic short-term reversal as liquidity-provision compensation: residualize each stock's recent move against the equal-weight market and fade only the stock-specific part, amplitude-weighted by the move's trailing-volatility z-score; dollar-neutral, multi-day holding.

Barbell cross-asset time-series momentum on a diversified 6-ETF basket (SPY,QQQ,IWM,TLT,GLD,HYG): per-asset trend = a barbell of a short (~20d) and long (~250-500d) fitted linear-trend slope sign, deliberately excluding the redundant medium (~125d) horizon; EMA-smoothed with hysteresis, mapped long-flat {0,+1}, inverse-vol (risk-parity) weighted with gross<=1 so net exposure is variable (cash when trend-flat). A timing-luck guard ensembles over several lookback pairs and staggered monthly rebalance offsets, averaging weights for robustness and low turnover. Exploits slow information diffusion/under-reaction; Sharpe edge from cross-asset trend diversification.

Turn-of-month seasonal tilt on SPY: hold SPY always but apply a small calendar-locked overweight during the TOM window (last trading day of prior month through TD+3, weighted toward days 0/+1), funded by a small underweight parked in a defensive TLT/GLD/HYG inverse-vol blend the rest of the month. Harvests the institutional-flow TOM anomaly as a mild oscillation, not an in/out switch, keeping the equity risk premium with only ~2 rebalances/month. Window fixed from theory; fully-invested rotation, no cash drag.

Cross-asset defensive asset allocation (DAA): monthly 13612W average-momentum scores rotate the risk book into the top-2 offensive equity ETFs (SPY/QQQ/IWM/XLK) and the defensive book into the best positive-momentum defensive asset (TLT/GLD/HYG, else cash). A canary breadth gate (SPY/HYG/IWM) sets cash fraction = share of canaries with non-positive momentum, de-risking before the held asset confirms a downtrend; positive-only defensive selection avoids the 2022 forced-long-bonds trap. Net exposure floats in [0,1]. An ensemble across staggered rebalance offsets (0/5/10/15) is averaged to neutralize rebalance timing luck and smooth turnover ~4x.

Cross-asset defensive asset allocation: 13612W front-weighted momentum ranks a risk sleeve (SPY/QQQ/IWM/XLK) and defensive sleeve (TLT/GLD/HYG); a SPY/HYG/IWM canary breadth gate sets the cash fraction so the book de-risks before the held asset confirms a downtrend; defensive assets bought only if their own momentum is positive (else cash) to avoid the 2022 forced-long-bond trap; net exposure floats in [0,1]; an ensemble across staggered monthly rebalance offsets (0/5/10/15) is averaged to neutralize rebalance timing luck and smooth turnover.

Turn-of-month seasonal tilt on SPY (overweight SPY in last-day/first-3-days window, park underweight in TLT/GLD/HYG inverse-vol blend). REJECTED: TOM seasonal premium statistically insignificant in every era (post-2015 +2.6bps/day, Welch t=0.48, p=0.63) and decayed from the pre-ETF era; loses to buy-and-hold SPY net of 10bps in EVERY sub-period (post-2015 Sharpe 0.793 vs SPY 0.817); amplitude sweep monotone toward tilt=0 (best variant is just hold SPY); no cost-robust variant beats SPY post-2015 at any amplitude incl 0.05; the -31% vs -34% maxDD edge is purely the static defensive parking (a constant non-seasonal defensive tilt does BETTER: maxDD -0.498, Sharpe 0.674), so the seasonal TIMING is value-destroying; ~130x SPY turnover; DSR fails (0.93) on full 2005+ sample at true n_trials=8.

Cross-asset DAA: 13612W momentum + canary breadth gate, top-2 offensive equity / top defensive (pos-mom only), timing-luck offset ensemble. Clean/robust/not-snooped but net Sharpe 0.83 LOSES to static 60/40 (1.00) on its own falsification bar; breadth gate adds ~0.01 Sharpe vs always-long-top2 (0.82); pos-def filter cosmetic; book is currently 100% QQQ+XLK tech-momentum long. REVISE: vol-target + duration sizing.

Barbell cross-asset TSMOM long-flat inverse-vol on 6-ETF basket. REJECTED: trend timing subtracts ~0.2 Sharpe vs an always-on inverse-vol RP version (0.80 vs 1.02) and worsens drawdown (-24.3% vs -21.6%); loses to EW-daily (0.99), InvVol-daily (1.05), InvVol-monthly (1.02), InvVol-static-buy&hold (0.94), and EW-buy&hold (0.93). Fails its own falsification rule (beat 6-ETF basket on Sharpe with lower DD). HYG concentration is a drag not edge (no-HYG=0.86, capped=0.91). 2022 crisis-convexity claim falsified (timing cost 2.9pp, still -19.6%). Genuinely good: clean leak scan, lowest turnover (0.007), cost-robust to 40bps, flat param plateau, ensemble cuts dispersion.

Predict sign of next-month SPY return from the variance risk premium (implied minus realized vol) at 1/3/6-month horizons via expanding-window walk-forward L2 logistic regression; trade long/flat SPY (long when P(up) beats expanding base rate, else cash). Large positive VRP is compensation for bearing vol risk and historically precedes positive equity returns (Bollerslev-Tauchen-Zhou 2009). Per-date standardization, 60-month min training window, no full-sample fitting; univariate VRP baseline as overfit guard.
VIX-RV variance risk premium predicts next-month SPY return sign (VIX-only)
